events.text: Something snarls out of the brush.
a rotted coin-purse half-buried in moss
a few coins spilled from some luckless traveller
a hoard-cache prised from beneath a toppled menhir
a moss-bed where weary travellers mend
a hidden pit-deadfall, its cover long rotted through
an old waystone, its rune worn to a coiled, scaled shape.
the wind carries a low note from the east, like something vast turning in its sleep.
a charcoal sketch nailed to a tree: a stair of stone descending into a great open mouth.
a scorched ring in the grass where no plant grows, the soil still faintly warm.
a ranger's cairn marking the dungeon road, three skulls set facing the deep as warning.
fishermen swear the vale lake has no bottom, and that on still nights it breathes.
